Login Issue – Unable to Access Waiver Pro Portal

text.text: Track and manage your support tickets.

While submitting a completed waiver application, the system displays an error message and prevents submission. All required fields appear to be filled. The issue occurs consistently when clicking the Submit Application button. User requests investigation into potential system or validation errors.

User from CDPH is unable to log into the Waiver Pro portal. After entering valid credentials, the system either refreshes the login page or shows an authentication error. The issue started today and persists across different browsers (Chrome and Edge). User requests assistance in verifying account status and resetting access if necessary.
Enter your email below to login to your account.
Streamline your regulatory waiver process from submission to approval

Apply when the facility is seeking a waiver of the 2.4 CNA requirement to meet individual patient needs but will provide a minimum of 3.5 direct care service hours. Facilities may only submit one Patient Needs Waiver and/or one Workforce Shortage Waiver application in the same year. If submitting both, only one may be approved.
Applies to individualized patient care plan modifications based on clinical needs
Documented patient or family request for accommodation required
Clinical assessment supporting the modification must be on file
Interdisciplinary team agreement and approval is required

Apply when the facility is seeking program flexibility to regulations. The request must include justification for the program flexibility, adequate description of the alternative concept, and documentation supporting that patient care will not be compromised. The facility may request program flexibility that is intended to be emergency or routine. An approved program flexibility request is for a limited term with a start and an end date. Facilities may submit more than one application as needed.
Submit a detailed justification demonstrating how the alternative maintains compliance and protects residents
All requests must be submitted online through WaiverPRO
CDPH will respond within 60 days after all required documentation is received
Approved flexibilities include an expiration date and specific terms; may be revoked if conditions are not met
This waiver was available from Dec 29, 2025 to Apr 30, 2026

Apply when the facility is seeking a waiver of the 3.5 direct care service hours requirement and/or the 2.4 CNA hours requirement due to a workforce shortage of available and appropriate health care professionals and direct care givers. Facilities may only submit one Patient Needs Waiver and/or one Workforce Shortage Waiver application in the same year. If submitting both, only one may be approved.
Facilities may not staff below 3.2 direct care hours per patient day, even with an approved waiver
Only licensed freestanding SNFs (excluding GACH distinct parts, state-owned hospitals, and developmental centers) may apply
Application Window: January 1 – April 1 annually; applications will not be accepted outside this timeframe
One Workforce Shortage Waiver and/or one Patient Needs Waiver per year; only one may be approved

Welcome to WaiverPro ("we," "our," or "us"). We are committed to protecting your privacy and ensuring the security of your personal information. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you use our waiver and program flexibility application management platform.
By accessing or using WaiverPro, you agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this policy. If you do not agree with our policies and practices, please do not use our service.
We collect information that you provide directly to us, including:
Name, email address, phone number, and contact information
Facility information, including facility name, address, and type
Waiver and program flexibility application data
Documentation and supporting materials uploaded to the platform
When you use WaiverPro, we automatically collect certain information, including:
Log data: IP address, browser type, operating system, pages visited, and time spent on pages
Device information: Device identifiers, device type, and mobile network information
Usage data: How you interact with the platform, features used, and performance metrics
We use the information we collect for the following purposes:
To provide, maintain, and improve our waiver management services
To process and manage waiver and program flexibility applications
To facilitate communication between facilities and regulatory authorities
To send you administrative information, updates, and notifications related to your applications
To respond to your inquiries, comments, and support requests
To monitor and analyze usage patterns and trends to improve our platform
To detect, prevent, and address technical issues, security threats, or fraudulent activity
To comply with legal obligations and regulatory requirements
We may share your information in the following circumstances:
With Regulatory Authorities: We may share application data and documentation with relevant regulatory bodies as part of the waiver review and approval process.
With Service Providers: We may share information with third-party service providers who perform services on our behalf, such as hosting, data storage, and customer support.
For Legal Compliance: We may disclose information if required by law, court order, or government regulation.
To Protect Rights: We may share information to protect our rights, privacy, safety, or property, or that of our users or others.
With Your Consent: We may share information with your explicit consent or at your direction.
We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal information to third parties for their marketing purposes.

We implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures to protect your personal information against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. These measures include:
Encryption of data in transit and at rest
Regular security assessments and vulnerability testing
Secure data centers with physical and environmental safeguards
Employee training on data protection and privacy practices

However, no method of transmission over the internet or electronic storage is 100% secure. While we strive to use commercially acceptable means to protect your information, we cannot guarantee absolute security.
You have certain rights regarding your personal information, including:
Access: You can request access to the personal information we hold about you
Correction: You can request correction of inaccurate or incomplete information
Deletion: You can request deletion of your personal information, subject to legal and contractual obligations
Data Portability: You can request a copy of your data in a portable format
Opt-Out: You can opt out of certain communications by following the unsubscribe instructions in our emails
To exercise these rights, please contact us using the information provided in the "Contact Us" section below.

We retain your personal information for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this Privacy Policy, unless a longer retention period is required or permitted by law. Application data and documentation may be retained in accordance with regulatory requirements and for historical record-keeping purposes.

WaiverPro is not intended for individuals under the age of 18. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If we become aware that we have collected personal information from a child without parental consent, we will take steps to delete such information.

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or for other operational, legal, or regulatory reasons. We will notify you of any material changes by posting the new Privacy Policy on this page and updating the "Last updated" date. We encourage you to review this Privacy Policy periodically.
If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us:
Address: 123 Healthcare Plaza, Suite 400, New York, NY 10001
By accessing or using WaiverPro ("the Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you disagree with any part of these terms, you may not access the Service.

These Terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you and WaiverPro. If you are using the Service on behalf of an organization, you represent and warrant that you have the authority to bind that organization to these Terms.

WaiverPro is a web-based platform designed to facilitate the management, submission, and tracking of healthcare waiver applications and program flexibility requests. The Service provides tools for facilities to:
Create and submit waiver and program flexibility applications
Track application status and review progress
To use certain features of the Service, you must register for an account. You agree to:
Provide accurate, current, and complete information during registration
Maintain and promptly update your account information
Maintain the security of your account credentials
Accept responsibility for all activities that occur under your account
Notify us immediately of any unauthorized use of your account

You must be at least 18 years old and have the legal capacity to enter into binding agreements to use the Service. By creating an account, you represent and warrant that you meet these eligibility requirements.
You agree to use the Service only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You agree not to:
Violate any applicable federal, state, local, or international law or regulation
Transmit any malicious code, viruses, or harmful materials
Attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Service or related systems
Interfere with or disrupt the Service or servers connected to the Service
Use automated systems or bots to access the Service without authorization
Impersonate any person or entity or misrepresent your affiliation
Submit false, fraudulent, or misleading information in applications

You are responsible for ensuring that all information submitted through the Service, including application data and documentation, is accurate, complete, and current. Submission of false or misleading information may result in application rejection, account termination, and legal consequences.

The Service and its original content, features, and functionality are owned by WaiverPro and are protected by international copyright, trademark, patent, trade secret, and other intellectual property laws.

You retain ownership of any content, data, or materials you submit through the Service ("User Content"). By submitting User Content, you grant WaiverPro a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to use, store, process, and display your User Content solely for the purpose of providing the Service, including:
Transmitting User Content to regulatory authorities as part of the application process
Displaying User Content to you and authorized users associated with your account
Storing and backing up User Content
WaiverPro provides a platform for submitting and managing waiver applications. Please note:
We facilitate the submission process but do not make decisions on application approval or denial
Application review, evaluation, and approval are conducted by relevant regulatory authorities
Processing times and outcomes are determined solely by regulatory bodies
We are not responsible for delays, rejections, or decisions made by regulatory authorities
You remain responsible for ensuring applications meet all regulatory requirements
Some features of the Service may be subject to fees. If applicable:
Fees will be clearly disclosed before you incur any charges
You agree to pay all fees associated with your use of paid features
Fees are non-refundable unless otherwise stated or required by law
We reserve the right to change fees with reasonable notice
Failure to pay fees may result in suspension or termination of your access to paid features
We strive to maintain high availability of the Service but do not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free operation. The Service may be unavailable due to:

We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue any aspect of the Service at any time, with or without notice. We are not liable for any losses or damages resulting from such modifications, suspensions, or discontinuations.

THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EITHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED. TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMISSIBLE BY LAW, WAIVERPRO DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO:

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, WAIVERPRO SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO:
Loss of profits, revenue, data, or use
Business interruption or loss of business opportunities
Damages arising from application rejections or regulatory decisions
Our total liability for any claims arising from or related to the Service shall not exceed the amount you paid to us in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.

You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless WaiverPro, its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, costs, or expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from your use of the Service, violation of these Terms, or infringement of any rights of another party.
We may terminate or suspend your account and access to the Service immediately, without prior notice, for any reason, including:
Submission of false or misleading information
Upon termination, your right to use the Service will cease immediately. We may delete your account data in accordance with our data retention policies, subject to legal and regulatory requirements.
These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

Any disputes arising from or related to these Terms or the Service shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association, except where prohibited by law.

We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Material changes will be notified by posting the updated Terms on this page and updating the "Last updated" date. Your continued use of the Service after such modifications constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms. If you do not agree to the modified Terms, you must stop using the Service.
If you have any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us:
Address: 123 Healthcare Plaza, Suite 400, New York, NY 10001
